title: Runtime Conformance Declaration
description: A runtime's declaration of which coordination model capabilities it supports. Used for interoperability testing and certification.

must.description: Required capabilities. All must be true for conformance.

abstract_operations.description: Support for all six abstract operations.

dynamic_lifecycle.description: Agents can be created and destroyed mid-task (not fixed rosters).

state_observability.description: Any agent's full state is inspectable by the coordinator.

budget_enforcement.description: Hard limits on agent count, cost, and time are enforced.

composable_patterns.description: Primitives can be nested (inner/outer) without framework changes.

trace_capture.description: Execution traces for any agent are capturable for distillation.

declarative_playbooks.description: Playbook configurations can be loaded and executed without code changes.

distributed_execution.description: Agents can run across multiple hosts.

tool_bindings.description: Optional: maps coordination model operations to native constructs in a specific AI coding tool. Used for tool-layer conformance documentation (see spec 035).

tool_name.description: Name of the AI coding tool being bound (e.g. 'claude-code', 'codex-cli').

tool_version.description: Version or release date of the tool assessed.

operation_binding.description: How a single coordination model operation maps to a tool's native construct.

tier.description: Conformance tier: full = native spec-compliant; partial = reduced semantics; emulated = documented workaround; unsupported = no viable path.

native_construct.description: The tool's native API, parameter, or pattern that implements this operation.

gap.description: Description of the semantic gap between the spec definition and the tool's implementation.

emulation_pattern.description: For emulated tier: brief description of the canonical workaround. See spec 035 for full patterns.
